justification: All 6 required rules identified (BR-1 through BR-6, plus BR-7 as a reinforcing rule for license verification). Regulatory rules (license verification BR-1/BR-7, record visibility BR-5, notes lock BR-6) correctly marked invariant: true. 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum of 4. Role-based cancellation correctly captured in BR-3 (patient) and BR-4 (admin) as separate rules. Confidence level 82 falls within the 65-90 range with clearly stated assumptions about license validation, temporal scope of access, and boundary conditions.

observations.text: Exceptional rule coverage — 7 rules identified with correct invariant classification, exceeding the 6 required
BR-3 (patient cancellation window) correctly classified as invariant: false with clear justification that the 2-hour threshold is configurable policy
BR-4 (admin-only late cancellation) correctly classified as invariant: false, recognizing the role boundary is authorization policy not a data integrity constraint
12 acceptance criteria with concrete values (e.g., 3 hours vs 1 hour, 47 hours vs 49 hours) making them immediately testable
AC-12 identifies concurrency as a testing concern for double-booking prevention — goes beyond the rubric requirements
Rich edge cases including boundary conditions (exactly 2 hours, adjacent appointments), license revocation, and null end_time scenarios
Confidence level of 82 appropriately reflects clear inputs while noting assumptions about license validation mechanism and temporal access scope
Escalations correctly flag the two most significant ambiguities (record access duration, license revocation) with concrete options and recommendations
